frame_analyses.description: Title slide: '@MIGS_TIPS KNOT TYING DROP NEEDLE TECHNIQUE' with J.J. Woo M.D. credit
Model setup showing Penrose drain with blue suture and two needle drivers visible. Drain positioned on pink practice board with target marks visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Drop needle technique setup. Two drivers ready. Blue suture clearly marked.

frame_analyses.description: Right needle driver positioning suture over the drain. Suture lies across the target slit area. Drivers positioned for first pass.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle driver controls suture trajectory. Drop needle technique uses needle driver to advance suture without needle driver grasping needle directly.

frame_analyses.description: Left driver now grasps suture to guide first pass through drain. Blue suture stretched across marked targets.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Coordinated two-hand control. Suture angle approaching perpendicular to drain axis.

frame_analyses.description: First pass in progress. Suture entering drain slit. Both drivers working in tandem to control suture entry angle and depth.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good control of entry angle. Suture passes cleanly through first mark. Appears to align with marked target line.

frame_analyses.description: Suture now passing through drain with right driver maintaining downward force. Blue loop visible on suture as first pass completes.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean pass. Suture trajectory remains controlled. No visible deviation from marked target.

frame_analyses.description: Second pass being set up. Right driver retracts, left driver repositions suture for return pass through second target mark.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth instrument exchange. Preparing for second mark penetration.

frame_analyses.description: Suture second pass proceeding. Drivers guide suture through second marked target. Blue suture creates loop pattern as shown.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Second mark engagement. Suture placement appears aligned with marked target region. Good perpendicular approach.

frame_analyses.description: First knot formation initiated. Suture ends held at tension by both drivers. Blue loop forming in extracorporeal space (outside trainer on model edge).
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Drop needle technique: knot tied outside the box using driver tips to manipulate suture. First throw configuration visible with red arrow annotation highlighting wrap direction.

frame_analyses.description: First single throw being cinched. Drivers working in opposition to tighten knot loop. Knot configuration tightening visibly.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Controlled cinching. Tension appears adequate but not excessive. Knot sits cleanly.

frame_analyses.description: Second throw being formed. Suture ends repositioned for second loop. Knot pusher not yet visible—throws being formed by driver manipulation alone.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Second loop formation. Wrapping pattern consistent with first throw. Clean geometry.

frame_analyses.description: Second throw cinched. Knot stack building. Left driver holds tension as right driver tightens second throw. Knots sitting at drain entry site.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Stacked knot appearance. Both throws appear secure. No visible slippage.

frame_analyses.description: Third throw being formed. Suture ends repositioned again. Pattern continues with alternating driver control.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final throw setup. Consistent wrapping technique across all three throws.

frame_analyses.description: Third throw cinched tightly. All three knots now stacked and secure at drain entry point. Knot stack appears well-formed with red arrow annotation highlighting final cinch.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot stack appears secure. Tension adequate. Drain slit region shows knots positioned at marked target area.

frame_analyses.description: Knot positioned at drain slit. Visual inspection shows knot sitting directly on marked slit closure point. Drain slit appears to be closed by knot tension.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Slit closure complete. Knot cinching has brought drain edges together. No visible gap at slit site.

frame_analyses.description: Scissors brought into frame. Black scissors positioned to cut suture ends proximal to knot stack.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Scissors approach. Suture ends still extending from knot. Positioning for clean cut.

frame_analyses.description: First suture end being cut. Scissors closing on suture segment. Cut appears to be taking place outside trainer (extracorporeal region).
frame_analyses.technique_notes: First cut in progress. Suture length cut appears reasonable.

frame_analyses.description: Second suture end cut. Both suture tails now severed. Knot remains secured at drain slit. Task approaching completion.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Both ends cut. Knot secure. Slit appears closed. Drain intact.

suture_placement.notes: Both target passes appear aligned with marked slit. Perpendicular entry angle maintained.

knot_assessments.notes: First throw cleanly formed and cinched. No visible slippage.
Second throw stacked cleanly on first. Consistent wrapping pattern.
Third throw completes knot stack. Final configuration appears stable.

drain_assessment.notes: Drain slit fully closed by knot tension. No visible gaps. Drain remains intact and properly positioned.

technique_assessment.description: Demonstrates drop needle technique for extracorporeal knot tying using needle driver tips to manipulate suture without grasping needle directly

confidence_rationale: This is an instructional/educational video with title cards, annotations (red/blue overlays), and teaching commentary embedded. It demonstrates the drop needle technique for task 4 but does not represent an actual timed performance. Completion time cannot be accurately determined as video appears edited with narration and instructional overlays. Cannot score as a performance attempt per FLS guidelines.
cannot_determine: Actual completion time - video is edited instructional content with pauses and annotations
Real-time performance assessment - this is a teaching demonstration, not a timed trainee performance
Whether knot pusher was used - video shows only driver manipulation; knot pusher not visible despite being required for task 4 specification
video_classification_note: This video is classified as 'instructional' rather than 'performance' because: (1) it contains title cards and educational branding (@MIGS_TIPS, J.J. Woo M.D.); (2) it includes graphical overlays (red/blue annotations highlighting technique); (3) intended purpose is teaching 'Drop Needle Technique' as stated in title; (4) video timing does not represent actual task performance timing.

demonstrated_techniques: Drop needle technique for extracorporeal knot tying
Suture introduction and target pass alignment
Extracorporeal loop formation using driver manipulation
Single throw cinching with opposing driver tension
common_errors_addressed: Proper perpendicular approach to avoid suture deviation
Clean driver-to-driver handoff without dropping suture
Adequate tension during knot cinching (neither too loose nor too tight)
Complete slit closure verification before cutting
Controlled suture cutting to avoid drain damage

technique_summary: Expert demonstration of drop needle technique for task 4 (extracorporeal suture with knot tying). All completion requirements met: suture passes through both marked targets, three single throws formed and cinched extracorporeally, drain slit fully closed, both suture ends cut. Technique is clean, efficient, and educationally valuable. Video is a teaching resource rather than a scoreable performance.
strengths: Clear visualization of drop needle technique using driver tips
Excellent suture placement alignment with marked targets
Consistent wrapping technique across all three throws
Complete drain slit closure with stable knot
improvement_suggestions_for_trainees: Practice coordinating both drivers simultaneously to maintain suture tension during knot formation
Develop muscle memory for driver positioning to speed up throw cycles
Ensure perpendicular entry angle on first pass to avoid deviation penalties
Verify knot security before cutting to prevent slippage during final suture cut
